title: The best movies on Netflix right now (April 2026)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Ralph Fiennes stars in '28 Years Later: The Bone Temple', now streaming on Netflix, continuing the '28 Years Later' saga.
'Atonement' starring Keira Knightley and James McAvoy is available on Netflix, showcasing Joe Wright's acclaimed 2007 period drama.
'Mission: Impossible' (1996), featuring Tom Cruise, is streaming on Netflix, offering the cerebral origin to the iconic action franchise.

title: Kelsey Fitzsimmons was found not guilty. Now she’s suing.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a former North Andover police officer, was acquitted of assault after being shot by a colleague last year.
Fitzsimmons plans to sue North Andover for civil rights and negligence following her acquittal of the assault charge.
Fitzsimmons's legal action focuses on the restraining order served by colleagues and the absence of police body cameras.

title: A church turned 6-BR home in Nahant — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: A converted church in Nahant, built in 1851, is now a 6-bedroom, 3-bath home with ocean views.
The property spans 5,969 square feet and includes an in-law apartment.
Potential buyers are asked to guess its price: $1.6 million, $1.8 million, or $2 million.

title: Should I DIY repair damage to a brand-new vehicle?
author: John Paul
bullets: A damaged side mirror on a 2026 Honda CR-V Sport can be fixed by DIY 3-D printing and epoxy.
DIY repair is possible, but leasing agreements require professional repair for damages beyond normal wear and tear.
Leaving the mirror damage untreated will not affect electronics, as they are robust against weather conditions.

title: A nude man was seen walking around a N.H. town overnight. Police would like to know who he is.
author: John Waller
bullets: Police in Bedford, NH, are searching for a nude man seen walking around neighborhoods early Thursday morning.
The man reportedly walked up driveways between 2 a.m. and 3 a.m. on Elizabeth Way and Connie Court.
The individual matches previous reports of a nude man on Campbell Road in recent weeks, police say.

title: This nurse is running Boston for MGH — and to honor her grandfather
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Abigail Zerbey, a 24-year-old nurse from North Andover, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for MGH.
Zerbey is honoring her grandfather, who received treatment at MGH for pancreatic cancer, by raising funds.
As a new nurse at MGH, she aims to support the hospital's patient care and pediatric cancer research programs.

title: Heat coach Erik Spoelstra credited Celtics’ ‘exceptional level of shot-making’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Erik Spoelstra praised the Celtics' shot-making after Miami's 147-129 loss, highlighting Boston's strong start with 53 first-quarter points.
Jaylen Brown and Sam Hauser led Boston's record-setting first quarter; Hauser was 5-for-5 from 3-point range during this period.
Boston excelled with nearly 60% shooting inside the arc, while Miami managed just 41.7% on 2-point attempts, according to Spoelstra.

title: 9 of the best baseball movies streaming now
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Nine of the best baseball movies are streaming in time for the 2026 Red Sox season opener.
Featured films include 'Field of Dreams', 'A League of Their Own', and 'The Natural'.
'Eephus', a Massachusetts-filmed movie with a 100% Rotten Tomatoes rating, is among the selections.

title: Mass. man charged with threatening to kill Trump, ‘hang him from the Statue of Liberty’
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Andrew Emerald, 45, charged with federal offenses for threatening President Donald Trump with violence via Facebook posts.
Emerald was arrested in Great Barrington after FBI agents found bladed weapons at his residence and he resisted arrest.
If convicted, Emerald faces up to five years in prison per count and a $250,000 fine, among other penalties.

title: 15-year-old Charlestown girl reported missing
author: John Waller
bullets: 15-year-old Hayden Fletcher is missing in Charlestown, last seen around 5:30 a.m. near 13th Street.
Hayden is described as 5-foot-4, 128 pounds, with blue eyes and dark blond hair, carrying a black bag with pink bows.
Boston Police urge anyone with information to contact 911 or Downtown Detectives at 617-343-4571. Tips can be anonymous.
